L1.area: Document generation and file output processing

L2.description: An insecure deserialization vulnerability exists where a case-insensitive PHP stream wrapper bypass allows an attacker to trigger deserialization of arbitrary archive files through the output file parameter. The existing protection only checks for a specific lowercase prefix string, but the wrapper protocol is case-insensitive, allowing uppercase or mixed-case variants to bypass the restriction and trigger object deserialization. Successful exploitation requires PHP 7 or below and allows arbitrary code execution.

L3.area: Abstract document generator output file preparation and validation
L3.description: An insecure deserialization vulnerability exists in the output file preparation logic where a case-sensitive string check for a PHP stream wrapper prefix can be bypassed using uppercase or mixed-case variants. Since PHP stream wrappers are case-insensitive, providing an uppercase version of the blocked wrapper prefix as the output filename allows triggering deserialization of arbitrary archive files. Successful exploitation requires PHP 7 or below and the ability to control the output filename parameter, allowing arbitrary code execution on the server.

ground_truth.reason: The prepareOutput function uses a case-sensitive string check for the 'phar://' wrapper prefix, which can be bypassed using uppercase variants like 'PHAR://' to trigger PHP archive deserialization and achieve remote code execution.
